meta.description: Base vs fine-tuned real GGUF inference comparison

case_results.note: Anaphylactic shock — SpO2 < 85 and SBP < 80 with HR > 130
Status epilepticus with fever — emergency discriminator
Drowning cardiac arrest — recent submersion confirmed, CPR in progress
Infant cardiac arrest — found 2 minutes ago, resuscitation indicated
Anaphylactic shock — SpO2 < 85 and SBP < 80 with HR > 130
Post-partum haemorrhage with haemorrhagic shock — SBP < 80 with HR > 130 and AVPU = U
Hanging cardiac arrest — actively being resuscitated, confirmed less than 5 minutes down
Acute stroke — very urgent discriminator, AVPU=V
Suspected SAH — very urgent discriminator, time-critical neurological emergency
Suspected ACS in female — atypical presentation
Severe hypoglycaemia — glucose < 3 and AVPU = V
Severe sepsis — AVPU = V, very urgent discriminator
Suspected ectopic rupture — very urgent, alert patient with haemodynamic instability
Suspected meningitis with sepsis — AVPU = V
Suspected aortic dissection — very urgent discriminator
Severe hypoglycaemia — glucose < 3 and AVPU = V
Acute stroke — within thrombolysis window, very urgent discriminator
Chest pain with stable vitals — borderline orange/yellow
Head injury GCS 14 — urgent discriminator, stable vitals
Chest pain with stable vitals — urgent discriminator
Moderate abdominal pain with stable vitals — urgent discriminator
Moderate pain with stable vitals — urgent discriminator
Febrile child, alert — RR 30 below urgent threshold, urgent discriminator
Pleuritic chest pain — not cardiac, stable vitals, urgent discriminator
Moderate pain in pregnancy with stable vitals — urgent discriminator
Minor wound — no discriminators, normal TEWS
Chronic cough — no red flags for TB, not urgent
Routine medication review — well-controlled hypertension, no urgency
Non-specific low back pain — no discriminators
Morning sickness — no complications, no discriminators
Expectant — end-stage cancer, actively dying, comfort care per family
Confirmed death — all BLUE criteria met
Expectant — end-stage HIV, actively dying, DNR per family
Confirmed death from unsurvivable traumatic injury
Confirmed death in advanced dementia patient
Expectant — terminal cancer, comfort care per advance directive
Paediatric drowning — prolonged submersion, confirmed death
Expectant — unsurvivable burns, no resuscitation indicated in any setting
Expectant in mass casualty — unsurvivable traumatic brain injury
Confirmed death at home — all BLUE criteria met
Expectant in mass casualty — unsurvivable blast injuries
Confirmed death with existing DNR order
Expectant in mass casualty — unsurvivable without unavailable surgery
